office_does: A state representative writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: Incumbent Alicia Rule runs on a mixed tax and housing record plus reproductive-rights and immigration positions; Misty Flowers emphasizes lower taxes, spending restraint, and outsider leadership.

candidates.summary: Rule is a third-term representative with a mixed supply-and-tenant-protection housing record and explicit reproductive-rights and immigration-enforcement positions.
highlights: Supports small-business tax cuts and opposed tax increases [S1]
Reports passing rent caps and zoning changes to increase supply [S1]
Supports abortion and IVF access and limits on masked ICE agents [S1]

taxes.basis: She supports small-business tax cuts and reports opposing tax increases.

housing.basis: Her record combines rent-increase caps with zoning changes intended to increase supply.

experience.basis: She is a third-term representative and former city councilmember.

immigration.basis: She supports limits on masked ICE agents and federal access to private data.

social.basis: She explicitly supports abortion and IVF access and replacement Planned Parenthood funding.

tech.basis: She supports data-privacy limits on federal authorities.

candidates.summary: Flowers is a small-business owner and first-time candidate emphasizing tax and spending restraint, public safety, and property protection.
highlights: Prioritizes public safety and property protection [S1]

taxes.basis: She explicitly supports lowering Washingtonians' tax burden.

spending.basis: She explicitly supports restraining state spending.

safety.basis: She emphasizes public safety and property protection, though mechanisms are broad.

experience.basis: She has no documented elected record.
